title: Opener library v1 — 5 scripts x v3/LatentSync (10 clips)

inputRefs.script: 5 opener scripts, 3.6-5.8 s each: hello / welcome-back / good-question / got-it / show-you
inputRefs.ttsReference: gs://openagentsgemini-oa-artifacts/sarah-avatar/voice-ref/sarah_voice_ref_v1.wav (clip 4); CosyVoice2 seed 1986 first-pass on all five

modelVersions.latentsync: 1.6 (per-clip -ls variants, full-band 24 kHz re-mux + CRF16 slow)

inputRefs.recipe: per script: one gated CosyVoice2 audio (seed 1986, loudnorm, per-segment whisper STT gate) rendered on BOTH the v3 recipe and LatentSync 1.6

sttRoundTrip.notes: word-level pass on all 10 clips — and still insufficient: the opener-01 'Hello?' rising-intonation defect passes word-level STT

pauseTiming.notes: robotic pacing on short scripts; zero-shot prosody instability dominates 4-6 s clips

prosody.notes: opener-01 rises ('Hello?') on the single most important word in the library; wrong intonation / robotic pacing / flat affect across the set — an opener IS its prosody. LLM audio judge (Gemini 1-10) adopted for the openers-v2 bake-off.

audioGates.initialismRisk: opener scripts avoid initialisms by design

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: OWNER VERDICT: ALL 10 clips fail playback — despite passing stills, jerk metrics, and word-level STT

avSync.notes: per-clip sync not separately measured; frame inspection surfaced no offset

cropSharpness.notes: 02/03/04 clean on inspection; opener-05 v3 motion (1.19) and jerk (0.14) suspiciously low for 4 s of continuous speech — possible under-articulation

temporalBoil.notes: no boil flagged in per-clip frame inspection

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: per-clip mouth-crop jerk: v3 variants 0.14-0.80 vs LatentSync 0.34-1.27 (v3 smoother on every script); LS variants inherit the 16-frame chunk hitch; openers 01/02 jerk inflated by real source motion, not artifacts

badFrameExclusions: openers 01/02 used high-motion big-smile source clips (mouth-region motion 3.7-4.2 vs 1.2 on the long take) — source-motion conflict; calm-clip rule (<1.5 mouth motion) adopted for openers-v2
videoGates.notes: Ranked failure hypotheses: zero-shot TTS prosody instability on short texts; source-motion conflict (synthesized mouth vs smiling cheeks/eyes); model-tier ceiling on expressiveness (neither MuseTalk nor LatentSync generates greeting-appropriate head/face motion).

operationalGates.costNotes: sum of per-clip renders: v3 443 s + LatentSync 881 s at ~$0.85/h, plus TTS

artifactExistenceCheck.notes: 10 MP4s + wavs + manifest.json verified in gs://…/sarah-avatar/openers/

hostDisposition.reason: prod_render_node — sarah-avatar-gpu-1 is the permanent OAV render node after the 2026-07-09 /sarah flip (~$0.85/h)

verdictSummary: OWNER PLAYBACK FAIL on ALL 10 clips despite passing stills, jerk metrics, and word-level STT. Hardened rules: playback + prosody judgment gate every take; short clips are the HARDER problem. Successors: openers-v2 audio bake-off (LLM judge), calm-clip re-render, Hallo2 still-animation tier.

title: sarah_reply_enhanced — hq + full-strength per-frame GFPGAN

inputRefs.sourceClip: footage clip 8 (v2_traced-df7d3b47…, 720p24), trimmed to frames 0-184 (7.71 s), cycled forward+reverse
inputRefs.script: OAV-1 Sarah reply (opener + C.1 product answer), same normalized-text audio as hq, 23.96 s

modelVersions.gfpgan: v1.4 full-strength per-frame on the 256^2 crop pre-paste-back

inputRefs.recipe: hq + GFPGAN v1.4 full-strength per-frame on the synthesized 256^2 crop before paste-back

pauseTiming.notes: staccato letter-speech pacing (same audio as hq)

prosody.notes: same staccato letter-spaced audio as hq; clipped audio likely amplified the clipped-viseme percept

audioGates.initialismRisk: normalizer letter-spacing 'A I' — correct words, robotic rhythm

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: OWNER VERDICT: choppier, plastic, way less humanlike in motion — after passing stills QA (crisp teeth, no measured flicker, invisible seam)

avSync.notes: not separately re-measured; frame inspection surfaced no offset

cropSharpness.notes: individual tooth definition, crisp lip edges — in stills; the sharpness is the problem in motion

temporalBoil.notes: no measured boil at sampled 24-frame windows (2.92 vs hq 2.82 mean diff at t=8 s) — but playback percept is per-frame GAN mode-snapping, invisible to frame-diff statistics

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: metrics statistically identical to the original — the owner-perceived choppiness is per-frame GAN restoration, not gross frame-level jitter

identityDrift.notes: no drift in stills; over-restored skin/teeth texture reads uncanny/plastic in motion

pasteBackSeam.notes: seam invisible in every sampled crop

videoGates.notes: The stills-vs-motion split that created the playback-first law: per-frame GFPGAN has no temporal model, so every frame is pulled toward its own restored mode.

hostDisposition.reason: prod_render_node — sarah-avatar-gpu-1 is the permanent OAV render node after the 2026-07-09 /sarah flip (~$0.85/h)

verdictSummary: OWNER PLAYBACK FAIL: choppier, plastic, less humanlike in motion — after passing stills QA. Full-strength per-frame GFPGAN is banned as a default; tamed recipe (v3) replaces it.

title: sarah_reply_hq (v2) — normalized-text audio + CRF16 encode

inputRefs.sourceClip: footage clip 8 (v2_traced-df7d3b47…, 720p24), trimmed to frames 0-184 (7.71 s), cycled forward+reverse
inputRefs.script: OAV-1 Sarah reply (opener + C.1 product answer), re-synthesized with spoken-form normalization, 23.96 s

modelVersions.encode: libx264 CRF16 preset slow (2.6 Mbps) + loudnorm

inputRefs.recipe: raw MuseTalk paste-back, no enhancement; normalized-text audio re-synthesis; CRF16 encode
commits: hydralisk 40b2783 (spoken-form TTS normalizer: initialism lexicon + all-caps heuristic)

sttRoundTrip.transcriber: Gemini (independent round-trip on the final mux)
sttRoundTrip.notes: 'A I' spoken as letters 3/3; 'coding agents' correct; all v1 defects fixed

pauseTiming.notes: staccato letter-speech pacing from hard 'A I' letter-spacing

prosody.notes: letter-spaced 'A I … A P I' reads staccato; punctuation-prosody variant ('A.I.') adopted in v3

audioGates.initialismRisk: normalizer letter-spacing 'A I' — correct words, robotic rhythm

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: no owner in-motion verdict recorded for hq alone; owner watched the enhanced derivative

avSync.notes: not separately re-measured; QA2 frame inspection surfaced no offset

cropSharpness.notes: encode softness gone at CRF16; residual MuseTalk 256^2 teeth softness remains

temporalBoil.notes: no flicker in QA2 sampled windows

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: whole-timeline mouth-region mean |diff| 8.88 vs original 8.77 (statistically identical); native-fps jerk metrics not computed for this take

pasteBackSeam.notes: blend seam invisible in every sampled crop

hostDisposition.reason: prod_render_node — sarah-avatar-gpu-1 is the permanent OAV render node after the 2026-07-09 /sarah flip (~$0.85/h)

verdictSummary: Audio defects from v1 all fixed (STT-verified); encode softness gone; residual MuseTalk 256^2 teeth softness. Baseline for the enhancement variants; no standalone owner playback verdict.

title: sarah_reply_latentsync — LatentSync 1.6 diffusion (offline quality tier)

inputRefs.sourceClip: footage clip 8 (v2_traced-df7d3b47…, 720p24), trimmed to frames 0-184 (7.71 s); output resampled to 25 fps (LatentSync training fps)
inputRefs.script: OAV-1 Sarah reply (opener + C.1 product answer), same v2 normalized-text audio, ~24 s

modelVersions.latentsync: 1.6 (512^2 lip-region diffusion, 20 steps, deepcache; Apache-2.0 verified in-repo)

inputRefs.recipe: ByteDance LatentSync 1.6 diffusion (D1); NOTE this take muxed the 16 kHz audio copy — final recipe must re-mux the full-band 24 kHz wav

sttRoundTrip.notes: same v2 audio; 16 kHz mux copy in this take — re-mux full-band wav in the final recipe

pauseTiming.notes: same staccato v2 letter-spaced audio as hq

prosody.notes: video looks natural DESPITE the staccato v2 audio — supporting the diagnosis that full-strength GFPGAN was the main motion problem in the enhanced take

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: head-to-head vs sarah_reply_v3 on the owner's Desktop pending; agent frame inspection called it the naturalness winner so far

avSync.notes: not separately re-measured; frame inspection surfaced no offset

cropSharpness.notes: teeth naturally soft (not GAN-crisp) — reads human, not plastic; 512^2 region

temporalBoil.notes: consecutive-frame check at t=8 s: smooth coarticulation, zero boil — its temporal layer works

metrics.periodicHitch: mean jerk by frame-index mod 16: phases 14-15 at 0.62/0.58 vs ~0.33 baseline — rhythmic hitch every 0.64 s at the 16-frame diffusion chunk boundary; upstream scripts/inference.py exposes no overlap/blending param, so mitigation needs pipeline surgery

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: ~2x jerk vs baseline even normalized for its wider articulation, and it is periodic

videoGates.notes: Best articulation of any take: clearly wider and more natural mouth shapes than MuseTalk (which under-articulates); rounded visemes present.

operationalGates.costNotes: 756 s render at ~$0.85/h (~31x realtime — offline recording tier only)

hostDisposition.reason: prod_render_node — sarah-avatar-gpu-1 is the permanent OAV render node after the 2026-07-09 /sarah flip (~$0.85/h)

verdictSummary: Best articulation of any take; blocked by the 16-frame chunk-boundary hitch (~2x jerk, periodic every 0.64 s). Offline/recording-tier candidate only, after chunk-seam smoothing.

title: sarah_reply v1 — MuseTalk 1.5 baseline (OAV-1 offline proof)

inputRefs.sourceClip: footage clip 8 (v2_traced-df7d3b47…, 720p24), trimmed to frames 0-184 (7.71 s), cycled forward+reverse
inputRefs.script: OAV-1 Sarah reply (opener + C.1 product answer, 371 chars, public-safe KB pitch), 21.84 s
inputRefs.recipe: raw MuseTalk paste-back, no enhancement; pre-normalizer audio

sttRoundTrip.notes: 'AI' rendered as 'eye'; 'coding agents' slurred to 'Cody's agents' (heard identically by both passes)

pauseTiming.notes: inter-sentence pauses slightly compressed/robotic (one continuous 371-char take); peak -0.2 dBFS near clipping, mean -18.5 dB — loudnorm added in hq

prosody.notes: energetic, clearly the reference voice; pacing slightly robotic between sentences (rated 8/10); no LLM judge yet in this era

audioGates.initialismRisk: no spoken-form normalizer yet — 'AI' spoken as 'eye'

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: no explicit owner in-motion verdict recorded; QA1 GO was agent frame inspection; superseded by the hq/enhanced/v3 ladder

avSync.notes: mouth state tracks speech onset/offset across sampled boundaries (QA1)

cropSharpness.notes: mouth soft/mushy: ~1.0 Mbps encode softness on top of the MuseTalk 256^2 ceiling; teeth a soft bright band at 2x zoom

temporalBoil.notes: zero single-frame jitter in consecutive-frame bursts (7.0 s burst; t=5 s and t=12 s tiles)

identityDrift.notes: pixel-identical to source outside the mouth crop (paste-back architecture)

pasteBackSeam.notes: no seam line, color shift, or chin discontinuity at any sampled timestamp incl. tilted head at t=18 s

badFrameExclusions: clip 8 frames 185-191 dropped (no detectable face — DWPose placeholder bbox crashes MuseTalk prep)
eyes-closed source pose at t≈18 s noted — speaking-state ranges must pin to eyes-open windows (OAV-2)

operationalGates.costNotes: full OAV-1 session ~7 h wall (~1.5 h busy) incl. env setup and two monitor-stall gaps; this render itself 119 s

artifactExistenceCheck.notes: monitors rewritten to artifact existence after two log-marker stall incidents (OAV-1 receipt §6.1)

hostDisposition.reason: staged for OAV-2/OAV-3 (host later became prod_render_node after the 2026-07-09 /sarah flip)

verdictSummary: Agent frame-QA GO on identity/sync (zero jitter, invisible seam); audio FAIL ('AI' as 'eye', 'coding' slur, -0.2 dBFS peak). Superseded by hq.

title: sarah_reply_v3 — tamed GFPGAN + punctuation-prosody audio

inputRefs.sourceClip: footage clip 8 (v2_traced-df7d3b47…, 720p24), trimmed to frames 0-184 (7.71 s), cycled forward+reverse
inputRefs.script: OAV-1 Sarah reply (opener + C.1 product answer), punctuation-prosody audio ('A.I.' replaces hard letter-spacing)

modelVersions.gfpgan: v1.4 tamed: alpha-blend 0.45 + feathered mouth-only mask + temporal EMA (pixels + bbox)

inputRefs.recipe: tamed GFPGAN (A2+A3): alpha 0.45 + feathered mouth mask + temporal EMA on the delta + audio_padding 3/3 + punctuation-prosody audio

sttRoundTrip.notes: all four 'A.I.' occurrences transcribe cleanly per-segment; whole-clip artifacts ('a AI', 'I.I.') were transcriber noise; 'coding agents' correct

pauseTiming.notes: improved rhythm via punctuation prosody; owner ear verdict pending

prosody.notes: punctuation-prosody 'A.I.' variant; owner ear verdict pending with the playback head-to-head

audioGates.initialismRisk: punctuation form 'A.I.' — natural rhythm, verified per-segment

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: head-to-head vs sarah_reply_latentsync on the owner's Desktop pending

avSync.notes: not separately re-measured; frame inspection surfaced no offset

cropSharpness.notes: teeth defined but natural — clearly between raw MuseTalk softness and plastic full-GFPGAN

temporalBoil.notes: consecutive frames at t=8 s smooth, no boil

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: measures smoother than every other take INCLUDING the original — the EMA + 0.45 blend + feathered mask recipe delivered

operationalGates.costNotes: 252 s render at ~$0.85/h (~3x faster than LatentSync)

hostDisposition.reason: prod_render_node — sarah-avatar-gpu-1 is the permanent OAV render node after the 2026-07-09 /sarah flip (~$0.85/h)

verdictSummary: Measured smoothest take of all (jerk mean 0.15, beating even the original); current best MuseTalk-lane recipe. Owner playback pending — cannot advance on metrics alone.
